description: The Institute for Supply Management releases the March 2026 Manufacturing PMI, a key indicator of industrial sector health.
The Bureau of Labor Statistics releases the March 2026 employment situation report, including non-farm payrolls and unemployment rate.
The Bureau of Labor Statistics publishes the Consumer Price Index for March 2026, measuring inflation trends across the US economy.
The Census Bureau releases advance estimates of US retail and food services sales for March 2026, indicating consumer spending trends.
The European Central Bank announces its monetary policy decision, which influences global capital flows and multinational earnings (estimated date based on typical 6-week cycle).
The Conference Board releases the Consumer Confidence Index for April 2026, reflecting household sentiment on economic conditions and spending intentions.
The Bureau of Economic Analysis publishes the advance estimate of Q1 2026 real GDP growth, providing the first comprehensive measure of economic output for the quarter.
The Bureau of Economic Analysis releases the Personal Consumption Expenditures Price Index for March 2026, the Federal Reserve's preferred measure of inflation.
The Institute for Supply Management releases the April 2026 Manufacturing PMI, indicating expansion or contraction in the manufacturing sector.
The Federal Open Market Committee announces its interest rate decision following the May 6-7 meeting, with an updated economic outlook and press conference.
The Bureau of Labor Statistics releases the April 2026 employment situation report, a key indicator of labor market strength.
The Bank of England's Monetary Policy Committee announces its interest rate decision, influencing global fixed-income markets and UK-exposed multinationals (estimated date based on typical schedule).
The Bureau of Labor Statistics publishes the Consumer Price Index for April 2026, providing the latest reading on inflation trends.
The Conference Board releases the Consumer Confidence Index for May 2026, gauging household expectations for income, business, and labor market conditions.

The Federal Reserve releases detailed minutes from the May 6-7 FOMC meeting, providing insight into policymakers' views on interest rates and the economic outlook (typically released 3 weeks after the meeting).
